title: selago hispida
intro: Selago hispida, also known as the hairy selago or hairy pygmy-pea, is a flowering plant species in the genus Selago, native to South Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds in its leaves and stems. The health benefits of Selago hispida include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Selago hispida include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
